'''Sid Domic''' (born {{birth date|1975|2|8|df=yes}}) is an Australian former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s and 2000s. He played in several positions for several clubs. Domic played in Australia for the [[Brisbane Broncos]] and the [[Penrith Panthers]], and in England for the [[London Broncos]], the [[Warrington Wolves]], the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]], and [[Hull FC]].<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Background==
Of [[Australian Aborigines|Aboriginal]] and [[Croatian people| Croatian]] descent, while attending [[North Rockhampton State High School]], Domic played for the [[Australia national schoolboys rugby league team|Australian Schoolboys team]] in 1991 and 1992.<ref>{{cite web|title=SportingPulse Homepage for Australian Secondary Schools Rugby League|url=http://www.sportingpulse.com/assoc_page.cgi?c=7-2130-0-0-0&sID=26424|access-date=10 October 2008|publisher=SportingPulse|archive-date=31 January 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170131030043/http://websites.sportstg.com/assoc_page.cgi?c=7-2130-0-0-0&sID=26424|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|author=<!--Staff writer(s)/no by-line.--> |title=NRL stars return to school|url=https://www.couriermail.com.au/news/queensland/rockhampton/sport/nrl-stars-return-to-school/news-story/5e2a1bb7413111735dd0a4f973eacc79|work=The Morning Bulletin|date=10 September 2012|access-date=26 July 2021}}</ref>

==Professional playing career==
Domic went on to play for the [[Brisbane Broncos]] and [[Penrith Panthers]] in the [[New South Wales Rugby League premiership]] and [[NRL]] before moving to England.

His [[Super League]] days have taken him from [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]], [[Wakefield Trinity Wildcats|Wakefield Trinity]] and [[Hull FC]]

Domic scored the winning try at the first local derby to be played in the [[Super League]] between Hull F.C. and their local rivals [[Hull Kingston Rovers]]. Hull F.C. reached the [[2006 Super League Grand final]] contested with [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] and Domic played as a {{rlp|ce}}, scoring his side's sole [[Try (rugby)|try]] in their 4–26 loss.

Domic was released from the [[Hull FC]] quota on 26 June to allow [[Mathew Head]] to play for the club.<ref name="Domic Released">{{cite web|url=http://www.hullfc.com/index.php?/news/more/domic-released/|title=Domic Released|publisher=hullfc.com|date=26 June 2007|access-date=26 June 2007|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070927222925/http://www.hullfc.com/index.php?%2Fnews%2Fmore%2Fdomic-released%2F|archive-date=27 September 2007|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name=" Half-back set Domic to leave Hull">{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/super_league/hull/6242012.stm|title= Half-back set Domic to leave Hull|publisher=BBC|date=26 June 2007|access-date=26 June 2007}}</ref>

In 2008, he joined the [[Dewsbury Rams]].

==Post-playing==
In 2010 Domic's artistic ability led him to be selected from a field of six artists to design the [[Indigenous All-Stars (rugby league)|Indigenous All Stars]]' jersey, as well as [[Johnathan Thurston]]'s custom headgear, for the annual [[All Stars Match|NRL All Stars]] matches.<ref>{{Cite news 
|last = Jodie van de Wetering and Kallee Buchanan
|title = All Stars' jerseys emblazoned with Domic's designs
|publisher = ABC News
|place = Australia
|date = 10 February 2010
|url = http://www.abc.net.au/local/stories/2010/02/10/2815754.htm
|access-date = 6 February 2011}}</ref> Much of his own knowledge in this area has been passed down to him from his grandmother, Ivy Domic. Through his fame from his playing career, he has visited children at many schools to introduce them to Aboriginal culture and his work has been exhibited at the Rebecca Hossack Gallery in the U.K.<ref name="BBC">{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/hull/5274204.stm|title=Domic's dreamtime designs|last=Shea|first=Julian|date=28 August 2006|publisher=BBC Sport|access-date=18 April 2010}}</ref>
